project.title: Show a low-stock hint in the inventory list without changing order imports.

target_files.message: Target file is verified enough to enter execution scope.

assumptions.text: Inventory list rows expose qty_available_units and replenishment_floor_units.
assumptions.message: Assumption is grounded by evidence or target project files.

question_trace.question: What do you want to improve or clarify?
question_trace.answer: 库存列表想加一个低库存提示，别影响下单导入。

question_trace.question: 我先不把任何文件当执行范围。需要确认：入口是不是库存列表、现有字段从哪里来、低库存判定规则、可见文案位置，以及下单导入保持不动。
question_trace.answer: 项目里字段不是普通 quantity，是 qty_available_units 和 replenishment_floor_units，小于等于补货线就是低库存。

question_trace.question: 我已读取 src/pages/inventory-list.tsx，确认字段是 qty_available_units 和 replenishment_floor_units；规则是 qty_available_units <= replenishment_floor_units。还缺 UI 文案、展示位置、样式来源、以及你接受的验收证据。
question_trace.answer: 文案就叫 Low stock，放在 SKU 后面，沿用项目现有 badge 样式，不新增颜色；截图或组件测试能看到 affected SKU 有 badge 就可以。

question_trace.question: 计划收敛为一个 UI 原子任务：只在库存列表 SKU 后显示 Low stock badge，不碰导入。现在需要你显式批准后才生成 PRD。
question_trace.answer: 确认，就按这个计划生成 PRD。

prd_intake.plain_language_problem: Show a low-stock hint in the inventory list without changing order imports.
desired_outcomes: Inventory list displays an inline 'Low stock' badge after the SKU when qty_available_units <= replenishment_floor_units.
success_proof: A screenshot or component test shows an inline 'Low stock' badge after the SKU when qty_available_units <= replenishment_floor_units.
boundaries: Do not change order import behavior.

nontechnical_intake.plain_language_problem: Show a low-stock hint in the inventory list without changing order imports.
current_workarounds: Managers only see raw inventory counts in the inventory list.
desired_outcomes: Inventory list displays an inline 'Low stock' badge after the SKU when qty_available_units <= replenishment_floor_units.
success_proof: A screenshot or component test shows an inline 'Low stock' badge after the SKU when qty_available_units <= replenishment_floor_units.
visual_style_source: Use the existing project badge style and do not introduce a new color system.
boundaries: Do not change order import behavior.
evidence: Agent read src/pages/inventory-list.tsx and confirmed it renders qty_available_units and replenishment_floor_units in inventory rows.
assumptions: Inventory list rows expose qty_available_units and replenishment_floor_units.

scenario_matrix.atomic_task_rule: one scenario surface becomes one session-sized task unless atomicity gate requires more splitting

checks.message: Vision must state the product direction or opportunity in enough detail.
Target user must be explicit before PRD work.
Status quo/current workaround must be captured.
Reflection must challenge premises, assumptions, or alternatives.
Investigation must record evidence or explicit assumptions/TBD-needs-validation.
Question trace or discussion rounds must capture the non-technical answers before PRD.
At least one confirmed requirement is required.
Every requirement should include at least one acceptance scenario.
Demand must be translated into a scenario matrix before executable PRD generation.
Every scenario must include explicit proof that a non-technical user can recognize.
Every scenario must map to at least one implementation surface for atomic task slicing.
Every scenario surface must declare session_budget.max_files <= 2 before executable PRD generation.
Roadmap must define MVP or phased delivery.
Blocking open questions must be resolved or explicitly deferred before PRD.
Explicit user approval with approved=true is required before executable PRD compilation.
Executable PRD requires target files/modules or a bounded execution scope.
Executable PRD requires project facts, field assumptions, and UI specs to be grounded before approval can proceed.

next_actions: Demand artifacts are ready for executable PRD compilation.

artifacts.description: Initial product vision, target user, problem, status quo, and opportunity hypothesis.
Premise challenge, assumptions, alternatives, and why this should continue.
Evidence, codebase scout, existing behavior, risks, and validation gaps.
Questioning rounds, answers, decisions, unresolved questions, and deferred ideas.
Demand quality gate, depth verification, and readiness level.
Confirmed requirements, acceptance scenarios, constraints, and out-of-scope boundaries.
Domain language, project context, current state, constraints, and durable decisions.
MVP, sequencing, later phases, dependencies, and risk-driven ordering.
Explicit human approval for PRD compilation and execution readiness.
Executable PRD compiled from approved demand artifacts.
